# shared/protocol/ — Claude operational rules

Read [`spec.md`](./spec.md) for layout, workflow, schema-evolution rules (1–10), and the cross-language test orchestration. This file captures the **operational** rules that aren't in spec.md — the things humans (and Claude) skip when they shouldn't.

## Never hand-edit generated files

`.proto` is SSOT. Every binding under `src/generated/`, `clients/*/app/src/main/java/com/moumantai/protocol/v1/`, and `clients/esp32/components/transport/generated/` is regenerated by `task protocol:gen`. Hand-edits will be overwritten and probably caught by `protocol:gen-check`.

## Workflow on every .proto change

```
1. Edit shared/protocol/proto/moumantai/v1/<file>.proto
2. task protocol:gen          # TS + Android Kotlin + Wear Kotlin + ESP32 C + SDK options
3. Review the diff            # all 4 language outputs should change in lockstep
4. task protocol:lint         # buf STANDARD rules
5. task protocol:test-cross-language   # TS + Kotlin byte-identical
6. task apps:typecheck        # apps submodule against generated SDK options
7. task <client>:build / typecheck     # consumers compile
8. Commit .proto + generated outputs together
```

`task protocol:breaking` enforces additive-only across the master baseline.

**Windows iteration shortcut:** the Wire/Gradle Kotlin codegen leg is slow on Windows. Use `task protocol:gen-fast` (TS + C only) while iterating; run the full `task protocol:gen` before committing so Android + Wear bindings stay in lockstep. `task protocol:gen-check` (CI) will fail the build if you forget.

## Silent-drop defenses (run all three before merging)

A proto field can be silently dropped at three points: (1) SDK option codegen omits it, (2) the factory doesn't pass it, (3) the renderer ignores it. Apps still compile and render — the field is dead with no warning.

- **`server/scripts/build-sdk-options.ts`** derives `*Options` + factory dispatch from `ComponentDefSchema`. Drift surfaces as a compile error in `apps:typecheck` (covers points 1–2).
- **`task apps:typecheck`** (`tsc --noEmit`). Fails when proto adds a field the generator forgot.
- **`task protocol:lint-coverage`** scans renderers for unhandled `ComponentDef` variants and dropped fields (covers point 3). Word-level matching has false negatives — reviewers compensate. Document intentional divergences in `scripts/coverage-allowlist.yaml`.

## Pre-merge checklist

For any PR touching `.proto`, `server/src/server/protocol/components/`, `shared/tokens/`, or renderer source:

1. `task design-system:gen-check` — design-system YAML / codegen / docs in lockstep
2. `task lint:tokens` — shared/tokens profile parity (catches YAML edits that diverge between compact/expanded)
3. `task protocol:gen-check` — proto bindings + SDK options up to date
4. `task protocol:lint` + `task protocol:format-check` — buf rules
5. `task protocol:test-cross-language` — fixture round-trip (TS + Android + Wear)
6. `task protocol:test-layout-resolution` — 5-leg layout conformance
7. `task protocol:lint-coverage` — renderer drift (allowlist entries must be documented)
8. `task protocol:lint-layout` — layout-imposing-token drift (renderer leaf files must route through resolver)
9. `task apps:typecheck` — apps submodule against codegen SDK
10. `task server:test` / `task pwa:test` / `task android:test` / `task wear-os:test`

**The `protocol-change-validator` subagent runs all 8 steps and reports drift — prefer it over running them by hand.** If a check fails post-merge, fix the drift; never bypass.

## Hard constraints (load-bearing)

- **Additive only.** Never remove a field or change its number; never repurpose a tag. `task protocol:breaking` (buf breaking) is the source of truth.
- **The 25-component `ComponentDef.component` oneof is locked.** New variants require coordinated `.proto` + every-renderer update across 4 languages. Add fields to existing components first (e.g. `body_kind` on `ScaffoldComponent`).
- **`optional` on every nullable scalar.** Distinguishes "unset" from "default-zero".
- **`[packed = true]` on every `repeated` of scalars / enums.** Wire's Kotlin default is non-packed; explicit `[packed=true]` keeps the wire form byte-identical across all 4 languages. `buf lint` does not catch this — cross-language fixture round-trip does.
- **Enum naming**: `SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE` prefixed with the enum name (`TURN_STATUS_COMPLETED`). The `0` value is always `*_UNSPECIFIED`.
- **No enum for LLM-authored values.** Protocol-controlled values (DeviceClass, DeviceShape, SizeClass, ChatRole, TurnStatus, VoiceStateValue, AudioFormat, ProtocolErrorCode, CloseCode, BinaryFrameType, BodyKind) are closed enums. Styling values (typography, font_weight, text_align, variant, image fit, alignments, keyboard_type, picker mode, progress variant, color, spacing) AND MIME types are free-form `string` so plugin apps + LLM-authored faces can use bespoke values without a schema bump.
- **Kotlin package via `option java_package = "com.moumantai.protocol.v1";`** in every file. Wire honors it; protobuf-es and nanopb ignore it.
